arts.ttl: Leeds United talent’s “athleticism” lauded after crucial moment in FA Cup giant killing
arts.sum: Leeds United’s out-on-loan midfielder Darko Gyabi was a key part in Plymouth Argyle’s shock FA Cup upset of Liverpool on Sunday afternoon, as the Pilgrims won 1-0. Darko Gyabi’s Plymouth spring huge FA Cup shock v Liverpool

arts.ttl: “One of the best fanbases” – Spaniard on Leeds United love and sport director plans
arts.sum: Diego Bermúdez has hailed Leeds United supporters as among the best during a chat with Spanish newspaper La Voz de Galicia. The Spaniard was part of Marcelo Bielsa’s backroom staff at Elland Road, working as an analyst. arts.ttl: Bamford written off but Leeds failure sparks Farke reminder as point made
arts.sum: “You know how highly I rate Patrick,” Farke has said on several occasions, also pointing to the fact that when Bamford is back, Leeds will have three fully fit strikers again. Farke clearly still values Bamford highly and so he might yet have a role to play in United’s promotion charge.

arts.ttl: Daniel Farke’s three transfer priorities proved right in Leeds United’s Millwall defeat
arts.sum: Daniel Farke’s sole focus this season is gaining automatic promotion back to the Premier League, but the chance to maintain a lengthy unbeaten run meant Leeds came into the FA Cup Fourth Round still eyeing victory over Millwall. Leeds United were knocked out of the FA Cup by Millwall on Saturday afternoon, deservedly beaten by a well-organised Alex Neil side.

arts.ttl: Five Watford players ruled out of Leeds United clash and one major fitness doubt
arts.sum: Leeds United head to play-off chasing Watford tomorrow night looking to return to winning ways. A late goal from Dennis Cirkin denied Watford maximum points at the Stadium of Light.

arts.ttl: Leeds United told incredible Premier League certainty likes of Sheffield United and Sunderland will hate
arts.sum: The probability of them finishing in the top two and winning automatic promotion is 95.2%, while the certainty they will go up one way or another is 98.5%, with the odds priced at 1/66 for Leeds to be a Premier League club next season. Leeds United will be playing Premier League football next season baring a miracle if the odds calculator and that ubiquitous supercomputer knows what they are talking about.

arts.ttl: Leeds United star aims brutal dig at Championship title rivals Sheffield United and Burnley
arts.sum: In an interview with Dutch outlet Algemeen Dagblad, the forward has claimed Leeds United play an attacking, aggressive brand of football compared to their fellow promotion hopefuls. “I feel completely at home at Leeds United," said Piroe.
